description_long: This object is a tall, slender obelisk constructed from what appears to be solid concrete, featuring a sharply pointed tip and a rectangular base. The surface is uniformly gray with subtle variations in tone, suggesting weathering or exposure to the elements, and has a matte, slightly rough finish. The sides are smooth and flat, tapering symmetrically from the wide base to the narrow apex, giving it a classic monolithic appearance. There are no visible inscriptions, engravings, or decorative motifs on any side, emphasizing its utilitarian and minimalist design. The base is broad and flat, providing stability, while the tip is sharply angled, forming a clean, geometric point. This obelisk would typically be found in urban environments such as parks, plazas, or as a boundary marker.
description: A tall, gray concrete obelisk with a sharply pointed tip and a broad, flat base, featuring a minimalist, unadorned design.
description_view_0: A narrow, triangular profile showing the sharply pointed tip and the full height of the obelisk.
description_view_1: A wide, flat face of the obelisk, displaying the broad rectangular base and the smooth, unadorned surface.
description_view_2: Another narrow, triangular profile identical to view 0, emphasizing the pointed tip and vertical symmetry.
description_view_3: A second wide, flat face mirroring view 1, showing the same broad base and plain surface.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, broad rectangular slab made of the same concrete material as the rest of the obelisk, with no visible steps, platforms, or decorative features.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is sharply pointed, forming a clean, geometric apex with no additional features or ornamentation.
